title: Harvard is Studying UFOs (Ft. Avi Loeb)
summary: Harvard astronomer Dr. Avi Loeb discusses his Galileo Project, which aims to use scientific instruments at Harvard's observatory to collect high-quality data on unidentified aerial phenomena (UAPs). The conversation covers the mysterious interstellar object Oumuamua that sparked Loeb's interest in extraterrestrial possibilities, the limitations of current government UFO data, and the scientific approach needed to study these phenomena.

acronyms.definition: Objects in the sky that cannot be explained, the modern term for UFOs used by the military and government
A government UFO investigation program that formed in 2007 and was run under the auspices of Bigelow Aerospace
Computer systems that can perform tasks requiring human intelligence, mentioned in context of analyzing UAP data and space exploration

events.description: Pentagon released a report acknowledging the existence of unidentified aerial phenomena on June 25th
Pan-STARRS telescope in Hawaii discovered the interstellar object Oumuamua
New York Times journalist Leslie Kean revealed the government had a UFO investigation program called ATIP
ATIP (Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program) was formed
Commander David Fravor saw the famous tic tac UFO off the coast of San Diego
Pan-STARRS found another baffling object that turned out to be a forgotten 1966 NASA rocket booster
Overwhelming series of UFO sightings in Washington DC in July, dubbed the invasion of Washington
The Condon Report closed shop on Project Blue Book and dismissed many findings with prosaic explanations
NASA mission launched a rocket booster that was later rediscovered in 2020

key_claims.claim: The interstellar object Oumuamua didn't fit any known criteria for asteroids or comets, lacked a cometary tail, and changed speed erratically
Oumuamua may have been a light sail - a thin structure that used the reflection of sunlight to propel itself forward
Military grade equipment is designed for the battlefield, not for the same quality instrumentation needed for scientific investigation of UAPs
The Galileo Project has more than 100 scientists engaged and is purchasing instrumentation to place on the roof of Harvard College Observatory
The James Webb Space Telescope could look for city lights on the night side of Proxima b
Technological equipment sent by extraterrestrials is much more likely to be our first encounter rather than living creatures because living beings cannot survive space exposed to cosmic rays
Virtual realities may be a solution to Fermi's paradox if advanced civilizations close themselves in cocoons living in virtual realities
The CIA wrote a memo saying UFO phenomena was a matter of national security and could touch off mass hysteria and panic after the 1952 Washington DC sightings
Eric Weinstein joined the Galileo Project
